$comment: Neutral shipped <utime.h> (POSIX) — c81 (the sqlite shell.c CLI route; shell.c:9841 [fileio.c] `#include <utime.h>` under !_WIN32). shell.c uses NOTHING from it (0 utime()/utimbuf uses — fileio's active branch calls the already-shipped utimes from <sys/time.h>); the include just has to RESOLVE. An empty descriptor fails loud by design ('declares nothing'), so this ships the header's honest MINIMAL real surface instead of a hollow marker: `struct utimbuf` + `utime()` — verified against the REAL /usr/include/utime.h in WSL (x86_64 + aarch64 cross headers identical, non-__USE_TIME_BITS64 LP64 default): actime __time_t(i64) @0, modtime __time_t(i64) @8 -> sizeof 16; `int utime(const char*, const struct utimbuf*)` with the struct param as ptr<void> per house style. Darwin agrees exactly (time_t i64, same two fields, utime in libSystem) -> [elf, macho], flat. libc symbol verified in WSL: utime@GLIBC_2.2.5 in libc.so.6.
